title: API Specification
status: Design intent. No API is implemented. Paths and shapes are indicative and will change on contact with a real client.
design_principles: REST over HTTPS, JSON. Boring on purpose. There is nothing in this domain that justifies a novel protocol.
Read-heavy. The dashboard and the webpage consuming this repository are the primary clients.
Every response that contains a number carries its provenance and its QC status. An API that returns bare numbers invites clients to render them as facts.
Tenant isolation is enforced at the API boundary, not in the client. A client bug must not be able to expose Farm B's data to Farm A.
Uncertainty is never optional in a response. If a client wants a scalar without an error bar, they can discard the field — but we will not omit it.

endpoints.returns: Sites visible to the authenticated tenant.
Site detail: geometry, units, stations, dock, commissioning record.
Cages, pens, or ponds with current state summary.
Fixed sampling stations. Profiles are only comparable across time if the station is the same place — this endpoint is what makes that guarantee inspectable.
Sortie records including aborts. Aborts are data, not noise — a pattern of weather aborts is the availability story.
Sortie detail with links to its profiles, imagery products, detections, and dispensing actions.
Profile records.
endpoints.notes: include_partial defaults to FALSE. Partial profiles are excluded from analysis by default because a truncated column silently corrupts any aggregate computed from it. A client must opt in to see them, which forces the client author to think about it.

endpoints.returns: Full profile with all samples and per-channel QC flags.
Thermocline depth, stratification index, oxygen debt, with inputs_complete flag.
A time series of one variable at one depth band.
endpoints.notes: qc_min defaults to 1 (good only). A client asking for suspect data must say so explicitly.

endpoints.returns: Imagery product metadata including solar elevation, illumination stability, and calibration reference.
Product with links to the raster and its MANDATORY uncertainty field.
endpoints.notes: A concentration product without an uncertainty field reference is a 500, not a partial response. It should be impossible to consume a concentration map without its errors.

endpoints.returns: Model detections with evidence references and human review verdicts.
Updated detection.
endpoints.notes: Human confirmation or rejection. Rejections are as valuable as confirmations — they are the training signal.

endpoints.returns: Twin state at a time, with per-layer provenance and staleness.
endpoints.notes: The response separates measured, interpolated, simulated, and operator-supplied into distinct objects. A client cannot accidentally blend them because the API does not offer a blended field.

endpoints.returns: Alerts with evidence references and the rule or model that raised them.
Acknowledgement record.
endpoints.notes: The acknowledge-to-action rate is a first-class product health metric. If people stop acting, the product has failed regardless of sensor accuracy.

endpoints.returns: Dispensing action records with commanded vs delivered mass and the human authoriser.
Authorisation token, single-use, mission-scoped.
endpoints.notes: The only write endpoint with real-world consequence. Requires an authenticated human principal — never a service account. The token does not persist beyond the mission it authorises.

endpoints.returns: Audit-ready environmental record with QC flags and provenance visible.
endpoints.notes: The certification audit use case. Deliberately includes the QC flags — an auditor should be able to see which readings we do not trust. Hiding them would make the report look better and be worth less.

endpoints.returns: Aircraft, dock, and sonde status including calibration due dates and sonde site assignment.
endpoints.notes: Sonde site assignment is a biosecurity control. If a sonde appears at a site it is not assigned to, that is an incident.

response_envelope.note: Every data response carries this envelope. It is the API-level expression of the repository's provenance discipline.

meta.provenance: verified_external | derived_calculation | modeled_projection | design_target | illustrative_synthetic | assumption
meta.staleness: age of the newest underlying measurement

shape.warnings: array of human-readable caveats the client SHOULD render

response_envelope.the_warnings_field: Clients are expected to render warnings, not swallow them. A warning like 'this concentration field was calibrated on 3 points with an R² of 0.41' is more important than the field itself, and an API that makes it easy to ignore is an API that facilitates overclaiming.

auth_and_tenancy.model: Per-tenant. A farm's data is visible only to that farm and to explicitly granted parties.
auth_and_tenancy.the_hard_case: The lake-scale carrying capacity use case (UC-10) needs cross-farm aggregation. The operator who benefits from the lake-wide picture is the one who least wants their own stocking visible in it.
auth_and_tenancy.position: Cross-tenant reads are not exposed by this API at all. Aggregated lake-scale products are a separate, explicitly-consented pipeline with their own governance. Building a cross-tenant read endpoint 'just for internal use' is how the boundary erodes.
auth_and_tenancy.status: UNRESOLVED. See ethics-data-privacy.json. Flagged because it is cheap to design now and impossible to retrofit.

what_this_api_will_not_do: Return a concentration without its uncertainty.
Return a model output in the same field as a measurement.
Accept a dispensing command without a human authoriser.
Expose one tenant's data to another, for any reason, including 'research'.
Silently include partial profiles in an aggregate.
Provide a person-detection or personnel-tracking endpoint. Not because it is technically hard — because building it would change what this company is.
